rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF JOSEPH PAUL DAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #21, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The CIU must be completed and filed and the assessment fee paid, if ordered, before the accounting can be approved. (2) In paragraph 13 of the petition, Conservator states that $2,000 in spending money was provided to the care home, but Exhibit A shows four receipts totaling $2,573.32. (3) 33.70 hours x $350 = $11,795.00. Please provide a (revised) proposed order (if appropriate) and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The only notice provided was the DE-115 Notice of Hearing on the Petition to Determine Claim to Property. This notice was given when the petition sought more than just a claim to the property, such as requesting the suspension of the personal representative and an accounting. It seems that the DE-120 Notice of Hearing must also be issued for the remaining requests. (2) A separate petition for appointment of a successor administrator that identifies a nominee, with the person's written consent. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KERN YEE WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/18/25. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of the hearing of this report has been filed. Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The pleading was not signed and verified by the Court-appointed fiduciary. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (4) A report of status of administration should include a petition requesting additional time to administer the estate. There is no prayer in this "status report," setting forth how much additional time the administrator with will annexed anticipates needing. [LRSF 14.35 J.2.] The report does not fully meet the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. (5) At the outset of these proceedings, the then petitioner amended her petition via a declaration (not an amendment or a supplement, as required by CRC 7.3), claiming that beneficiary Bryan Wong's successors-in-interest are his wife and a minor daughter. Did he die intestate? Who represents the minor daughter's interest? No guardian ad litem has been appointed for her. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please do not "answer" issues like a test. A supplement or amendment to a pleading should stand on its own and either provide additional information (supplement) or correct allegations (amendment). If a proof of service (or other document) was not filed, it should just be filed and not attached to a supplement or amendment to a petition. Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., Betty Wong, Administrator with Will Annexed. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE RICHARD BULLETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The attorney represents the administrator. Did the administrator consent to his attorney now representing a party who would be entitled to the entire estate? (2) What is Lisa Cleveland's relationship to the decedent? Item 8 shows "Sole Beneficiary," which is not a relationship, which should be either some kinship or stranger. (3) A Proof of Holographic Instrument is more persuasive if signed by a disinterested party. [LRSF 14.18 A.] Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Unless both parties consent to the dual representation and someone other than the petitioner/sole beneficiary verifies a Proof of Holographic Instrument, an appearance will be required after issues are addressed.)
